description: Following the collapse of the Tokyo-based Mt. Gox exchange in February 2014, which lost 850,000 Bitcoins, public sentiment toward the viability and security of cryptocurrency plummeted globally. This event marked the first major existential crisis for Bitcoin, forcing a long-term shift in institutional skepticism regarding decentralized financial platforms.

key_events.description: Bitcoin reaches record highs above $1,000 on optimism.
Mt. Gox suspends all withdrawals citing technical issues.
Mt. Gox files for bankruptcy; CEO Mark Karpelès faces public scrutiny.
Global regulators issue warnings against using digital currencies.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling the 'crash-induced' shift from speculative optimism to extreme regulatory caution.
